original_hypothesis.title: setBufferConfig Applies New Replenishment Rate Retroactively to Pending Period
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _setBufferConfig, the new buffer config values (max, threshold, replenishRateInBasis) are written to storage BEFORE buffer.update(block.number) is called. The update's calcPendingBuffer uses the newly stored values to calculate buffer changes for the period since the last update (prevBlockNumber). This means the new replenishment rate is applied retroactively to the entire elapsed period. If the rate increases dramatically (e.g., from 100 to 10000 basis points), the buffer is over-replenished for the historical period. If decreased, under-replenished.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Configuration changes should apply prospectively, not retroactively. Applying new rates to historical periods produces incorrect buffer values.

proof_of_signal.pattern: buffer.max, buffer.threshold, buffer.replenishRateInBasis updated before buffer.update(block.number); calcPendingBuffer reads self.replenishRateInBasis (now new value) for elapsed since self.prevBlockNumber

preconditions: Significant time elapsed since last buffer update

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Storage updated before buffer.update call; calcPendingBuffer uses newly stored values for historical period calculation
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires rollup owner to call setBufferConfig
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: During any buffer config change when system is synced

operational_questions: How much buffer error can result from typical config changes?
Could an admin exploit this to manipulate buffer state?
Is the buffer.update conditional (synced only) sufficient mitigation?
validation_requirements: Calculate buffer error for realistic config change scenarios
Test buffer state before and after config change with different rates

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Wait for significant time to elapse without buffer updates
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: buffer.update applies high rate retroactively, over-replenishing buffer

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Buffer values incorrect; force inclusion delays affected

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Buffer config changes followed by unexpected buffer value jumps
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Buffer value discontinuities after setBufferConfig calls

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
